content: Motor learning and sport psychology misapplication pitfalls: (1) Physical motor learning research (Fitts stages, contextual interference, blocked vs random practice) was developed for physical skill acquisition — direct transfer to RTS game mechanics (mouse/keyboard) is unverified. (2) Esports coaching research is sparse (acknowledged in literature); WC3-specific research essentially does not exist — overclaiming research backing is a credibility risk. (3) Neuromyths are common in coaching contexts: left/right brain dominance, 10% brain usage, learning styles (VAK) — all discredited but widely cited. (4) Deliberate practice requires immediate corrective feedback — replays partially fulfill this but WC3 has no equivalent of a coach watching in real-time. (5) Specificity principle: skills practiced in isolation may not transfer to actual game conditions without game-context practice. (6) Citing studies without validating that the study population, task type, and conditions map to WC3 players reads as pseudo-intellectual name-dropping.
